title: insert_note_with_witness: add sync warning for SQL duplication
description:
insert_note_with_witness() in wallet/src/db.rs contains two-branch INSERT SQL (with/without plaintext columns) that is identical to the SQL in insert_note(). If the notes table schema changes in a future migration, a maintainer must update BOTH functions — but there is no warning comment communicating this. When the next column is added (e.g., account_index), the risk of updating one but not the other is high.

## Fix
Add a warning comment directly above the INSERT SQL in insert_note_with_witness() that says:
- This SQL is intentionally duplicated from insert_note()
- Both must be updated together if the notes table schema changes
- Reference insert_note() by line number or function name

Also add a brief note in insert_note() itself pointing back to insert_note_with_witness().

No code logic changes — documentation only.
acceptance_criteria: cargo clippy -p nie-wallet -- -D warnings passes. The comment exists in both insert_note() and insert_note_with_witness(). No test changes needed.
